engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz CLC Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The OM646 evolved from the OM611, bringing common-rail injection and variable geometry turbocharging to the CLC range for refined, efficient diesel power .
compatibleVariants: CLC 200 CDI, CLC 200 CDI Sport, CLC 200 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY, CLC 220 CDI, CLC 220 CDI Sport, CLC 220 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY
commonFailures: Swirl flap collapse - intake flaps break and can be ingested into the engine
Injector seal leakage causing diesel combustion knock
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM646 Mercedes Benz CLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mercedes-Benz’s 72-degree V6 turbodiesel represented a leap in power density and refinement, delivering V8-like torque with four-cylinder economy when introduced .
commonFailures: Oil cooler seal leak - orange seals harden and leak; upgraded Viton (purple) seals required
Swirl flap motor failure - integral to intake manifold, costly replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz CLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Supercharged Petrol
entries.history: The M271 four-cylinder combined supercharging, variable valve timing, and a balance shaft for six-cylinder smoothness in a smaller package. It powered Mercedes’ core entry-level models throughout the 2000s .
compatibleVariants: CLC 160, CLC 160 Sport, CLC 180 Kompressor, CLC 180 Kompressor Sport, CLC 200 Kompressor, CLC 200 Kompressor Sport
commonFailures: Timing chain and tensioner failure - single-row chain stretches (reported from 45,000 miles)
Supercharger coupler wear causing rattling noise
entries.cta: Get quotes for M271 Mercedes Benz CLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Direct Injection Petrol (CGI)
entries.history: The CGI (Stratified-Charged Gasoline Injection) variant of the M271 introduced direct injection to Mercedes’ four-cylinder range, improving fuel economy and torque delivery.
commonFailures: Intake valve carbon build-up - direct injection deposits (typical from 50,000 miles)
Timing chain stretch (shared with supercharged M271)
entries.cta: Get quotes for M271 CGI Mercedes Benz CLC Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L / 3.5L Petrol V6
entries.history: The M272 V6 replaced the M112 with all-aluminium construction, continuous VVT on both camshafts, and advanced coolant management - delivering smooth, linear power across the rev range .
compatibleVariants: CLC 230, CLC 230 Sport, CLC 350, CLC 350 Sport
commonFailures: Balance shaft gear wear - affected engines with serial numbers below 2729..30 468993 (2004-2008 production); causes timing faults and check engine light
Intake manifold tumble flap linkage failure - plastic levers break, requiring complete manifold replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272 Mercedes Benz CLC Class engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can’t find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we’ll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Mercedes Benz CLC Class engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLC Class M271 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLC Class M271 Timing Chain Failure - Why Petrol Engines Rattle on Startup
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLC Class CLC 160, CLC 180 Kompressor, CLC 200 Kompressor, CLC 200 CGI (2008-2011, M271.910, M271.946, M271.950, M271.860)
problems.rootCause: The M271 uses a single-row timing chain that stretches over time, combined with a plastic tensioner guide that loses hydraulic pressure. Cold starts produce a distinctive rattle as the chain slaps against the timing cover. If ignored, the chain can jump teeth, causing valve-to-piston contact and complete engine destruction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of timing chain, tensioner, guides, and camshaft sprockets. Labour-intensive (8-10 hours)
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles. Only viable before chain jumps. Essential preventative maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M271 with upgraded timing components, new oil pump, and bench testing
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles. The right choice if chain has jumped or engine has suffered valve damage

problems.recommendation: On a CLC Class worth £1,500-£4,000, preventative timing chain replacement is almost always the better value if caught early. Listen for cold-start rattle - the moment you hear it, book a specialist inspection. If the chain has already jumped, replacement is your only safe option. A specialist using upgraded timing components will outlast the original design.
problems.cta: Compare M271 timing chain replacement quotes for your Mercedes Benz CLC Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLC Class OM646 Swirl Flap Collapse
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLC Class OM646 Swirl Flap Collapse - The #1 Diesel Killer
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLC Class CLC 200 CDI, CLC 220 CDI, CLC 220 CDI BlueEFFICIENCY (2008-2011, OM646.980, OM646.981)
problems.rootCause: The OM646's inlet manifold contains plastic swirl flaps that regulate airflow for emissions. Over time, EGR carbon buildup causes flaps to seize, and the plastic pivot points can snap . A broken flap can be ingested directly into a cylinder, causing catastrophic piston, valve, and cylinder head damage .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of all flaps, blanking of pivot holes in the manifold, ECU remap to delete fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution. Eliminates future flap failure risk entirely. May affect emissions compliance post-2010

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM646 with swirl flap delete or upgraded manifold
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent. Only required if flap ingestion has destroyed the cylinder head or pistons

problems.recommendation: The moment a swirl flap fault code appears (often accompanied by limp mode or a hissing intake sound ), arrange flap removal immediately. Dealer pricing for a new manifold is prohibitive at £1,200+. Specialist flap removal and blanking costs a fraction of that and permanently solves the issue. For pre-2010 CLC Class cars, this is the sensible long-term fix.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM646 swirl flap repair on your Mercedes Benz CLC Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLC Class M272 Balance Shaft Wear
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLC Class M272 Balance Shaft Wear - The V6 Time Bomb
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLC Class CLC 230, CLC 230 Sport, CLC 350, CLC 350 Sport (2008-2011, M272.921, M272.960)
problems.rootCause: Early M272 engines (with serial numbers below 2729..30 468993) used a balance shaft gear manufactured from a soft metal alloy . The gear teeth wear down prematurely, causing slack in the timing chain and incorrect camshaft timing. Fault codes P0016, P0017, or P0021-P0022 typically appear .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of balance shaft with upgraded hardened gear, plus timing chain, tensioners, and guides. Engine-out job
repairOptions.longevity: 80,000+ miles. The correct repair for early detection. Parts are permanently upgraded

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M272 with upgraded balance shaft components and full warrantied assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent. Required if timing slip has caused valve damage

problems.recommendation: This repair is expensive because the balance shaft sits deep within the engine, requiring either engine removal or extensive front-end disassembly . On a CLC 350 worth £3,000-£5,000, the specialist balance shaft replacement is worth doing if the car is otherwise sound. On a higher-mileage CLC 230, consider whether replacement makes more sense. If the engine has already jumped timing, replacement is usually the economical choice.
problems.cta: Compare M272 balance shaft repair costs for your Mercedes Benz CLC Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLC Class OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Leak
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLC Class OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Leak - The V6 Diesel Weep
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLC Class CLC 320 CDI, CLC 320 CDI Sport (2008-2011, OM642.852)
problems.rootCause: The OM642 V6 diesel uses orange rubber oil cooler seals that harden and shrink with heat cycles. Once they fail, engine oil leaks externally (visible as drips under the car) or internally into the cooling system . Mercedes later released upgraded purple Viton seals to address the issue .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of oil cooler seals with upgraded Viton units, plus intake manifold removal, oil cooler clean, full coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent with upgraded seals. Labour-intensive due to manifold removal (7-9 hours)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt OM642 with upgraded oil cooler seals, new turbo lines, and full bench test
repairOptions.longevity: Required if neglected leak has caused oil starvation, turbo failure, or severe engine damage

problems.recommendation: Oil cooler seal failure is not immediately catastrophic, but it creates a persistent, messy leak that worsens over time. Check your driveway for oil drips and your coolant reservoir for oily residue. Specialist replacement with genuine Mercedes upgraded seals is the right fix. Do not use aftermarket seals . If left for 20,000+ miles, low oil pressure can damage the turbocharger and main bearings - then replacement becomes the cheaper option.
problems.cta: Get OM642 oil cooler repair quotes for your Mercedes Benz CLC Class

problems.group: Mercedes Benz CLC Class M271 Camshaft Adjuster Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz CLC Class M271 Camshaft Adjuster Failure - The VVT Rattle That Won't Quit
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Benz CLC Class CLC 160, CLC 180 Kompressor, CLC 200 Kompressor (2008-2011, M271.910, M271.946, M271.950)
problems.rootCause: The M271's camshaft adjusters (VVT phasers) rely on oil pressure to vary cam timing. Internally, the locking mechanism can wear, causing a loud rattling noise from the top of the engine that persists beyond the initial cold start - distinct from chain tensioner rattle. Fault codes P0016, P0017, or P0025 confirm the issue .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of one or both camshaft adjusters, timing chain, tensioner, and guides. Requires timing tools
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles. Often done alongside timing chain replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt M271 with new adjusters, upgraded timing components, and full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if adjuster failure has caused valve timing errors leading to piston contact

problems.recommendation: If your M271 petrol CLC rattles constantly from the top end - not just at cold start - have a specialist diagnose the adjusters. Replacing them alongside the timing chain is cost-effective labour-wise as the timing cover is already off. On a well-maintained CLC 180 or 200 Kompressor, this repair gives you another 60,000+ miles of reliable service.
problems.cta: Compare M271 camshaft adjuster repair quotes for your Mercedes Benz CLC Class

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz CLC Class
finalCta.paragraph: Your CLC Class is a niche model - finding the right specialist with experience on the CL203 platform matters. EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Mercedes specialists who understand M271 timing chains, OM646 swirl flaps, M272 balance shafts, and OM642 oil cooler seals. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network includes upgraded components designed to eliminate the original failure point, plus a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices, choose a specialist, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz CLC Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz CLC Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz CLC Class is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years, power outputs and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1-litre OM646 turbodiesel was the sole diesel displacement offered across the entire CLC Class range from 2008 to 2011. It powered the high-volume CLC 200 CDI and CLC 220 CDI variants, with two power outputs sharing the same basic block and common-rail injection architecture. This engine represented Mercedes' push for efficiency in the compact executive segment, delivering strong torque and respectable economy.
commonFailurePoints: injector seal leakage causing combustion knock
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLC Class 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre OM642 V6 turbodiesel was the flagship diesel option for the CLC Class, reserved exclusively for the CLC 320 CDI and its Sport trim variant. This 72-degree V6 delivered 224 hp and substantial torque, transforming the CLC into a genuine long-distance tourer. However, the engine's complexity and tight packaging within the CL203 engine bay make maintenance and replacement more labour-intensive than the four-cylinder OM646.
commonFailurePoints: Oil cooler seal leakage (orange seals harden
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLC Class 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre M271 four-cylinder formed the backbone of the CLC Class petrol range, appearing in three distinct forms across the lineup. Supercharged variants powered the CLC 160, CLC 180 Kompressor and CLC 200 Kompressor, while the direct-injection CGI version appeared in the CLC 200 CGI. This engine family defined the petrol CLC experience - responsive supercharged delivery in the Kompressor cars, with the CGI offering slightly better economy.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLC Class 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre M272 V6 sat as the entry point into the CLC Class six-cylinder petrol range, available exclusively in the CLC 230 and CLC 230 Sport. With 204 hp and a naturally aspirated character, it offered a step up in refinement and smoothness over the four-cylinder M271 without the running costs of the range-topping 3.5-litre. The V6 configuration also delivered a more premium driving experience, though the additional complexity within the CL203 bay increases labour time for major repairs.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear (engines below serial 2729..30 468993)
intake manifold tumble flap linkage breakage
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLC Class 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz CLC Class 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.5-litre M272 V6 was the ultimate petrol engine fitted to the CLC Class, reserved for the CLC 350 and CLC 350 Sport. Producing 272 hp, it made the CLC genuinely quick, propelling the compact coupe to 60 mph in just over six seconds. This flagship petrol unit shares many design traits with the 2.5-litre M272 but features a longer stroke and revised internals to achieve the larger displacement, along with a more aggressive engine calibration. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz CLC Class needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz CLC Class specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear (pre-2008 production engines)
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz CLC Class 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.descriptor: The CLC Class diesel range relied exclusively on Mercedes' OM646 four-cylinder and OM642 V6 units, with the 2.1-litre four-cylinder dominating UK replacement enquiries due to swirl flap failures.

items.descriptor: The CLC Class petrol lineup spans supercharged M271 four-cylinders and naturally aspirated M272 V6 units, with timing chain failures on the 1.8L and balance shaft wear on the 2.5L/3.5L driving most replacement enquiries.

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: The Mercedes Benz CLC Class was produced as a single generation (CL203) from 2008 to 2011, evolving through running changes rather than generational breaks - this timeline covers every engine fitted across that period.

years.description: The CLC debuted in August 2008 as a heavily updated version of the C-Class Sports Coupe (CL203), featuring over 1,100 new components and a revised engine lineup based entirely around Mercedes' four-cylinder M271 and V6 M272 units .
keyChanges: CL203 platform (previously C-Class Sports Coupe, 2001-2008) fully refreshed for CLC branding
M271 four-cylinder petrol engines offered with both supercharged (Kompressor) and direct-injection (CGI) configurations
OM646 four-cylinder diesel introduced as the efficiency-focused option
M272 V6 petrol and OM642 V6 diesel reserved for flagship variants
No AMG variants produced for CLC generation
Production ended June 2011 with final sales continuing through 2011
knownFor: Single-generation model bridging the C-Class Sports Coupe and CLC branding
M271 timing chain tensioner failures drive most petrol replacement enquiries
OM646 swirl flap collapse is the #1 diesel failure, often requiring full replacement
M272 balance shaft wear affects V6 engines with serial numbers below 2729..30 468993
Lower entry price than equivalent C-Class made it a popular used buy
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2011 Mercedes Benz CLC Class replacement engines
years.ticker: M271 timing chain replacement CLC 200 Kompressor • OM646 swirl flap failure CLC 220 CDI • M272 balance shaft repair CLC 350 • OM642 oil cooler seal leak CLC 320 CDI • CLC 180 Kompressor engine rebuild cost
enquiries: M271 timing chain replacement CLC 200 Kompressor
OM646 swirl flap failure CLC 220 CDI
OM642 oil cooler seal leak CLC 320 CDI

years.description: From 2009, Mercedes expanded the CLC's engine range with fuel-efficiency-focused BlueEFFICIENCY diesel variants and the direct-injection M271 CGI petrol unit.
keyChanges: CLC 160 BlueEFFICIENCY introduced with 1.6L M271.910 engine (129hp)
BlueEFFICIENCY diesel variants appeared on CLC 200 CDI and CLC 220 CDI
CLC 200 CGI (direct-injection petrol) added as alternative to supercharged Kompressor
Fuel economy improvements across the range to meet tightening Euro 5 standards
Special Edition trim introduced briefly from July 2009 to October 2010
knownFor: CLC 160 offered the lowest running costs in the petrol range
CGI direct injection brought improved fuel economy but carbon build-up risk on intake valves
BlueEFFICIENCY diesel variants achieved up to 55mpg combined
1.6L M271.911 shares M271 family timing chain weakness despite smaller displacement
Short production window makes these variants less common on used market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2011 Mercedes Benz CLC Class BlueEFFICIENCY & CGI engines
years.ticker: CLC 160 BlueEFFICIENCY engine replacement • CLC 200 CGI carbon clean cost • OM646 swirl flap delete BlueEFFICIENCY

years.description: The supercharged M271 Kompressor engines formed the core of the CLC petrol lineup, with 1.8L units producing between 143hp and 184hp across CLC 180 and CLC 200 variants.
keyChanges: M271.946 (143hp) fitted to CLC 180 Kompressor
M271.950 (184hp) fitted to CLC 200 Kompressor
Five-speed automatic transmission standard on Australian/Kompressor models
Supercharger (Eaton M45) provides responsive low-end torque
Sport trim added for both Kompressor variants with revised suspension
knownFor: Responsive supercharged delivery character - distinct from naturally aspirated rivals
Single-row timing chain tensioner failure is the most common engine-related repair
Supercharger coupler wear produces rattling noise from front of engine
CLC 200 Kompressor remains the highest-volume petrol variant for replacement enquiries
Power delivery smooth with 250Nm torque from 2,800-5,000rpm
years.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz CLC Class Kompressor supercharged engine replacement
years.ticker: CLC 200 Kompressor timing chain replacement • M271 supercharger coupler repair • CLC 180 Kompressor engine rebuild cost • M271 camshaft adjuster failure CLC Kompressor
enquiries: M271 camshaft adjuster failure CLC Kompressor

years.description: The CLC's V6 offerings - the M272 petrol in 2.5L and 3.5L forms, plus the OM642 3.0L V6 diesel - represented the performance flagships of the range, though each carries well-documented failure points .
keyChanges: M272.921 2.5L V6 (204hp) exclusive to CLC 230
M272.960 3.5L V6 (272hp) fitted to CLC 350 / CLC 350 Sport
OM642.852 3.0L V6 diesel (224hp) exclusive to CLC 320 CDI
All V6 variants paired with 7G-Tronic automatic transmission
Sport trim on V6 cars included stiffer suspension and revised styling
knownFor: M272 balance shaft gear wear affects engines produced before 2008
OM642 oil cooler seal leak is the dominant V6 diesel failure requiring replacement
V6 petrol cars offer smooth, linear power delivery with premium refinement
Engine-out repairs required for balance shaft work due to V6 packaging in CL203 bay
CLC 350 Sport is the rarest and most desirable CLC variant

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz CLC Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What are the most common engine problems on the Mercedes CLC Class?
items.answer: The most frequent CLC engine failures are M271 timing chain stretch (petrol) and OM646 swirl flap collapse (diesel). Both can destroy the engine if ignored. M272 V6 balance shaft wear also affects 2008 CLC 230/350 models.
keyPoints: M271 petrol engines fail when the single-row timing chain stretches (45k-80k miles)
OM646 diesel swirl flaps break and fall into cylinders - immediate engine damage risk
M272 V6 balance shaft gear wears on engines with serial numbers below 2729..30 468993
Early warning signs: rattling on cold start (petrol) or hissing intake noise (diesel)
Preventative timing chain replacement costs less than half of a full engine rebuild
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes CLC Class engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: How much does a Mercedes CLC Class engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt CLC engine prices range from £1,200 for a 1.8L M271 petrol to £4,200 for a 3.5L M272 V6. OM646 diesel units typically cost £1,400-£3,200. Supply and fit adds £500-£900 depending on the variant.
keyPoints: M271 1.8L petrol engines (CLC 160/180/200 Kompressor): £1,200-£2,800
OM646 2.1L diesel engines (CLC 200/220 CDI): £1,400-£3,200
All rebuilt engines on EngineMarket include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mercedes CLC Class engine prices - enter your reg for exact quotes

items.question: Which CLC Class engine is the most reliable - petrol or diesel?
items.answer: The OM646 2.1L diesel is generally more robust than the M271 petrol, but only if you address swirl flap failure preventatively. The M272 V6 petrol is reliable once the balance shaft issue is fixed. The M271 petrol's timing chain is its weak point.
keyPoints: OM646 diesel: swirl flap collapse is the main risk - delete flaps preventatively to eliminate it
OM646 engines reach 200k-400k miles with regular oil changes (MB229.5 spec)
M271 petrol: budget for timing chain replacement at 60k-80k miles as preventative maintenance
M272 V6 petrol: check engine serial number - balance shaft was upgraded mid-2008
None have catastrophic DPF or AdBlue issues - diesel is safe for mixed driving
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Mercedes CLC Class engine with warranty - diesel or petrol

items.question: Is a CLC 180 Kompressor or CLC 200 Kompressor worth repairing when the timing chain fails?
items.answer: Yes - a specialist timing chain replacement costs £800-£1,200 and returns the M271 engine to reliable service. Only replace the whole engine if the chain has jumped and damaged valves. A CLC worth £2,000-£4,000 justifies the repair.
keyPoints: Preventative timing chain replacement (before failure): £800-£1,200 at a specialist
Full engine replacement after valve damage: £1,800-£3,200 supply only
Upgraded timing components in rebuilt engines outlast the original design
Cars with full service history (MB229.5 oil) are better candidates for repair
Get a specialist inspection - if compression is good, timing chain repair is the smart choice
items.cta: Get quotes for M271 timing chain replacement on your Mercedes CLC Class

items.question: What causes OM646 swirl flap failure on the CLC 220 CDI - and can it be fixed permanently?
items.answer: Plastic swirl flaps in the intake manifold break from carbon buildup and heat cycles. They can drop into cylinders, destroying pistons and valves. The permanent fix is swirl flap removal and ECU mapping - not replacement with another plastic manifold.
keyPoints: Swirl flaps fail between 70k-120k miles as plastic pivot points wear
Dealer solution (£1,200+ new manifold) uses the same flawed plastic parts
Specialist flap removal and blanking (£400-£800) eliminates the failure permanently
Pre-2010 CLC models can have flaps removed legally - no MOT emissions issues
If flap ingestion already happened, you need a rebuilt OM646 engine (£1,500-£3,200)
items.cta: Get OM646 swirl flap delete quotes for your Mercedes CLC Class diesel

items.question: Does the CLC 320 CDI (OM642 V6 diesel) have engine problems I should know about?
items.answer: The OM642 V6's most common failure is oil cooler seal leaks - orange rubber seals harden and leak externally or into the coolant. Replace with upgraded purple Viton seals. Timing chains and injectors are more robust than on four-cylinder Mercedes diesels.
keyPoints: Oil cooler seal failure occurs at 70k-110k miles - watch for oil drips or oily coolant
Specialist replacement with upgraded seals: £700-£1,200
Turbo actuator failure also common on higher-mileage OM642 units
The V6 diesel is otherwise reliable and smoother than OM646 four-cylinder
If ignored, oil leaks cause low pressure and turbo damage - then replacement is needed
items.cta: Get OM642 oil cooler repair quotes for your CLC 320 CDI

items.question: What engine codes were fitted to the Mercedes CLC Class - and which should I avoid?
items.answer: CLC engines: M271 (1.8L petrol) in CLC 160/180/200 Kompressor and 200 CGI, M272 V6 in CLC 230/350, OM646 (2.1L diesel) in CLC 200/220 CDI, OM642 V6 diesel in CLC 320 CDI. None are "avoid at all costs" - each has a known weakness with a proven fix.
keyPoints: M271 (petrol) - weakness: timing chain stretch (fix: upgraded tensioner and chain)
OM646 (diesel) - weakness: swirl flap collapse (fix: flap delete and blanking)
M272 (V6 petrol) - weakness: balance shaft wear (fix: upgraded gear, check engine serial)
OM642 (V6 diesel) - weakness: oil cooler seals (fix: upgraded purple Viton seals)
All CLC engines share the same CL203 platform - no generation-specific surprises
items.cta: Compare prices by engine code - enter your reg to identify your CLC's exact unit

items.question: How long do Mercedes CLC Class engines last - and what maintenance extends their life?
items.answer: A well-maintained OM646 diesel can reach 200k-400k miles. M271 petrol typically lasts 120k-180k miles but needs timing chain replacement around 60k-80k miles. The single most important factor is using Mercedes MB229.5 specification oil changed every 9,000 miles or 12 months.
keyPoints: OM646 diesel longevity: 200k-400k miles with regular oil changes using MB229.5
M271 petrol longevity: 120k-180k miles - timing chain is the limiting component
Service every 12 months/9,000 miles alternating between Service A and Service B
Coolant replacement every 15 years if original factory-filled long-life coolant
On M271 engines, check camshaft position sensor plugs for oil - oil in wiring damages ECU
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes CLC Class replacement engines - budget for preventative maintenance

items.question: Can I fit a used CLC engine myself - or should I use a specialist?
items.answer: A used engine may save money upfront but carries the same design flaws that killed your original unit. Specialists fitting rebuilt engines include upgraded components (timing chain, swirl flap delete) that prevent repeat failure. DIY fitment also risks immobiliser/ECU coding issues on CL203 platform cars.
keyPoints: Used engines: £800-£2,200 but no upgraded components - original failure risks remain
Rebuilt engines: £1,200-£4,200 with upgraded parts addressing the original failure point
CL203 platform requires ECU coding - a used ECU may need immobiliser synchronisation
Specialist supply & fit: £500-£900 added cost but includes warranty on parts AND labour
M271 timing chain replacement requires special timing tools - not a driveway job
items.cta: Find supply & fit deals for Mercedes CLC Class engines - nationwide network

items.question: Is a rebuilt or reconditioned CLC engine better than a used one?
items.answer: A rebuilt or reconditioned engine is almost always better value for long-term ownership. Used engines are cheaper upfront (£800-£2,200) but carry the same M271 timing chain or OM646 swirl flap risks. Rebuilt units (£1,200-£4,200) include upgraded components and a 12-month warranty.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: full strip-down, new pistons/rings/bearings - closest to new specification
Rebuilt: focused replacement of failed components + upgraded parts at the failure point
Used: lowest upfront cost but no warranty on internal condition or hidden damage
Only rebuilt engines include upgraded M271 timing components or OM646 swirl flap delete
For a car you plan to keep 2+ years, the extra £600-£1,200 for rebuilt pays for itself
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt vs used Mercedes CLC Class engine prices now
